Want to shape the conversation? Pick a Speaker
==============================================

Pick a Speaker is our open call for meaningful voices and industry-defining debates. Submit a talk you want to give, propose a panel, or nominate someone you think should be on stage at Web Summit Vancouver.

**Please note: Applications are closed now. Successful applicants will be contacted soon.**

[Book tickets](/tickets/attendees/)

![Joey Camire, Chief Strategy Officer, SYLVAIN on Marketing Summit Stage during day one of Web Summit 2024](https://vancouver.websummit.com/wp-media/2026/01/Joey-Camire-Chief-Strategy-Officer-SYLVAIN-on-Marketing-Summit-Stage-during-day-one-of-Web-Summit-2024.jpg)

Joey Camire,  
Co-founder and CEO  
SYLVAIN

The voices we want to elevate and spotlight
-------------------------------------------

The people driving meaningful change are often found far beyond the corner office. They are experts in their fields, asking and answering the questions that will define the next decade.

With **Pick a Speaker**, we want to ensure they have access to our global stage by opening up applications to our community. Don’t let vital discussions stay in the hallways.

![Gary Marcus, Professor, scientist, bestselling author, entrepreneur, and AI contrarian; Amy Nordrum, Executive Editor, MIT Technology Review, on centre stage during opening night at Web Summit Vancouver 2025](https://vancouver.websummit.com/wp-media/2025/06/Opening-Night-Vancouver-2025-center-stage.jpg)

How you can take part
---------------------

Whether it is your voice or the voice of a mentor, Pick a Speaker gives you the chance to:

*   **Pitch a talk you are ready to give**  
    Share a bold idea, breakthrough, or perspective shaping your industry.
*   **Propose a panel tackling the tough questions**  
    Bring together voices that challenge assumptions and spark meaningful debate.
*   **Nominate a visionary the world has not heard from yet**  
    Put forward someone whose work deserves a global stage.

![Edith Yeung, Race Capital](https://vancouver.websummit.com/wp-media/2025/09/Edith-Yeung-Race-Capital.jpg)

Pick a Speaker FAQs
-------------------

##### **When does Pick a Speaker open and close?**

Submissions open 10 February at 9:00am (Vancouver time) and close on 26 February at 11:00pm (Vancouver time). We recommend getting your submission in early to avoid any last-minute rush.

##### **When will selected speakers be announced?**

We’ll announce the selected talks, panels and nominated speakers the week commencing 13 April.

##### **What can I submit?**

You can choose from three routes:

*   *   *   submit a talk – Put forward an idea, insight or story you want to share with our audience.
        *   submit a panel – Propose a topic and outline the speakers you’d like involved.
        *   nominate someone else – Spot a great voice or expert? Put them forward to speak in Vancouver.

##### **Who can apply?**

Anyone with strong insight, relevant experience or a story that adds value to our audience. Whether you’re a first-time speaker or a seasoned storyteller, we’re open to hearing from you.

##### **What makes a strong submission?**

Clear and specific ideas work best. Strong submissions usually:

*   *   *   focus on one well-defined message
        *   highlight insight rather than self-promotion
        *   connect to themes that matter to the Web Summit Vancouver audience
        *   show why you (or the nominated speaker) are the right voice for this topic
        *   add to the diversity of perspectives on our stages

##### **Can I submit more than one idea?**

Yes. If you have multiple talks or panels in mind, you’re welcome to submit each one separately.

##### **Can I nominate someone without asking them?**

We recommend checking with the person first. If they’re selected to speak, they’ll need to be available for next steps.

##### **Do I need a full panel confirmed before submitting?**

No. For panel submissions, you can either list confirmed speakers or outline the types of contributors you’d like. Our content team may help shape the final lineup.

##### **Is there a fee to submit?**

No. There’s no cost to submit a talk, panel or nomination.If selected, speakers receive an event pass for Web Summit Vancouver. Travel and accommodation are not included unless stated otherwise.

##### **How are speakers selected?**

Our content team reviews every submission. We look at originality, clarity, depth of insight, relevance to Vancouver’s programme and how the idea fits within the wider content themes across the event.

##### **When will I hear back?**

You’ll hear from us before the public announcement in April. If your submission progresses, we’ll reach out directly with next steps.

##### **Who can I contact if I have a question?**

If your question isn’t answered here, email [pickaspeaker@websummit.com](mailto:pickaspeaker@websummit.com) and we’ll be happy to help.

##### **Ready to join a global community of storytellers and innovators? Apply now and join us in Vancouver.**

About us
--------

In the years since 2009, Web Summit has grown from a 150-person conference in Dublin to a global event series gathering more than 140,000 attendees every year from all over the world. We build incredible things, from technology and stages to relationships and experiences. Since 2009, we've grown our team to more than 340 people. At our headquarters in Dublin, Ireland – and in Portugal, Canada, Qatar, Brazil and many more locations around the world – incredible engineers, developers, producers, designers, marketers and salespeople come together to create unique global events. In Europe, North America, South America, Asia and the Middle East, we are builders of world-class global technology festivals. Our events take on the flavour of their surroundings, while still retaining the successful framework we've fine-tuned over the years. Web Summit events have become unrivalled global meeting places for the world's most disruptive technology companies, and for those interested in how that disruption can transform their businesses and their lives. Help us build great things: https://websummit.com/careers
